figures.caption: Figure 3: Left: Compression vs. quality Pareto frontier. Rank 128 achieves the best PPL at 11.7× compression. Right: GPU memory by method. Even rank 256 saves 40% of VRAM.
Figure 2: Loss convergence for all ranks. All SCT configurations converge to the same loss floor (∼4.2–4.5) regardless of rank. Dense converges to 1.29.
Table 3: Rank sweep results. Loss and PPL are smoothed (window=50). Rank 128 (bold) achieves the best PPL among SCT configurations.
Table 2: 70B architecture validation on consumer hardware. Both platforms complete a full training step under 8 GB.
Figure 1: Training memory at 70B scale. SCT requires 172× less memory than dense training.
Table 1: Per-MLP-layer training memory (weights + gradients + Adam states) at rank 32.
